role_summary: Civil rights movement organizer; in this corpus's embedding paired with Kay Tillow (entry 74, possible family or partnership), and his Tactical Pragmatism vs Strategic Vision position is +1.00, the MAXIMUM Strategic Vision reading in the entire 136-interview corpus.
ai_reading: Tillow sits at +1.00 on the Tactical Pragmatism vs Strategic Vision axis, the absolute maximum reading toward Strategic Vision in the catalog. The cultural placement of Tillow would need to be exceptionally strategic-vision-oriented (a multi-decade policy analyst, a deeply theoretical organizer) for this reading not to be an AI-vs-culture surprise; without external biographical sourcing in this iteration the catalog notes the structural extremum without resolving the question. His top semantic neighbor is Lisa Anderson Todd (entry 79, count 176), with Charles McLaurin (entry 17), Courtland Cox (entry 24), Ekwueme Michael Thelwell (entry 34), and his paired-name Kay Tillow (entry 74, count 121) rounding out the cluster. The Kay Tillow pairing is the catalog's second-most-explicit family-or-partner cosine signature after the Aaron-Elmer Dixon brother pairing. The embedding reads Walter's testimony as deeply intertwined with Kay Tillow's, suggesting they organized in close partnership across the periods their interviews discuss.
biographical_paragraph: Walter Tillow's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 124 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
